content.full_text:
B Addressed Letter Keplinger 2601
Confirmation
letter                                                              T/A

August 4, 1976

A -76-603

Mr. Jack R. Weatley
2240 Cowper
Palo Alto, California  94301

Dear Mr. Weatley:

Your letter of July 9, 1976 to Senator Arlen Gregorio
has been referred to this agency for comment.

The Political Reform Act of 1974, which requires the
disclosure of campaign contributions and expenditures, pro-
vides that the State Franchise Tax Board shall conduct audits
and field investigations in regard to campaign statements and
reports.

The Political Reform Act does not require the Franchise
Tax Board's Political Reform Program to send "confirmation
letters" to contributors such as yourself.  The Franchise Tax
Board, however, considers such letters to be a necessary and
routine part of an audit.

It should be noted that compliance with the Franchise
Tax Board's Political Reform Program request for information
verifying a contribution is entirely voluntary on the part of
the recipient of such a "confirmation letter."  We hope you
were not unduly inconvenienced.

Sincerely,


JOHN KEPLINGER
Division Chief

JK:bw

cc:  Senator Arlen Gregorio
bcc: Gina
     Jeanne P.

[This document appears to be a pink/faded typed page with text that is largely illegible due to the image quality and fading. Most of the content cannot be read with confidence. There are some visible elements:]

AIT
[multiple paragraphs of illegible text]

[illegible]

[At bottom of page:]
[illegible]
[illegible]

[The document has two holes punched near the top, suggesting it was part of a bound document. The pink/faded coloring and poor contrast make most of the typewritten text impossible to transcribe accurately.]

Sacramento Address:
State Capitol
95814
(916) 445-0870

District Office Address:
1177 University Drive, Suite 2
Menlo Park, California 94025
(415) 323-8448

Senator
Arlen Gregorio

Tenth District
San Mateo and Santa Clara Counties

Committees
Health and Welfare
Chairman
Education
Elections and Reapportionment
Governmental Organization

July 28, 1976

Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Enclosed is a letter from a constituent of Senator Gregorio relative to the necessity of his having to file the amount of his political contributions to the FPPC and what he believes to be duplication of effort.

Would you be good enough to address a reply directly to him regarding this requirement. Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Beth A. Labson
Administrative Assistant

BAL: rw

I can see this is a mostly blank document with only two small curved marks or symbols visible near the top of the page. The rest of the page appears to be empty white space.

[illegible]

Sacramento Address:
State Capitol
95814
(916) 445-0870

District Office Address:
1177 University Drive, Suite 2
Menlo Park, California 94025
(415) 323-8418

Committees
Health and Welfare
Chairman
Education
Elections and Reapportionment
Governmental Organization

Senator

Arlen Gregorio
Tenth District

San Mateo and Santa Clara Counties

July 28, 1976

Mr. Jack R. Wheatley
2240 Cowper
Palo Alto, California 94301

Dear Mr. Wheatley:

Senator Gregorio has asked me to thank you for your recent letter
regarding the Political Audit Program reply. I
have sent a copy of your letter to the Fair Political Practices
Commission in order to inform them of your concern. The FPPC,
as you know, was created by Proposition 9 of the 1974 ballot.
The people of the state voted overwhelmingly to create the
Commission.

The Senator has long been a supporter of the concepts of this
Proposition. He would like to see any inefficiencies eliminated
from the process, but he believes that the Commission provides
the people with invaluable information on candidates. He firmly
believes that the FPPC helps to increase the accountability of
candidates and elected officials.

He appreciates having the benefit of your views. I hope you
will continue to share with him your concerns on matters
currently before the Legislature.

Sincerely,

Beth A. Labson
Administrative Assistant

BAL:nw

I can see this image appears to be mostly blank or very faint, with only what looks like two small circular marks or holes visible near the top of the page. The rest of the document appears to be empty white space.

Since there is no legible text content to transcribe, I cannot provide a text transcription following your rules.

Jack R. Wheatley
2240 Cowper
Palo Alto, Ca. 94301

July 9, 1976


The Honorable Arlen Gregorio
State Senator
Senate Post Office
State Capitol
Sacramento, Cal. 95814

Dear Senator Gregorio:

I have received a form letter from the Political Reform Audit
Program requesting a listing of contributions made to the
Republican Central Committee of Santa Clara County for the
period of January 7, 1975 through December 31, 1975.

I am complying with this request under protest. Every law
enacted seems to trigger a new avalanche of paperwork in its
wake. The Candidates and their Committees already submit
a report showing name, address and business of contributors.
This is just one more report wasting the taxpayers money and
time and discouraging political participation by responsible
citizens.

Very truly yours,

Jack R. Wheatley

am

I can see this is a mostly blank document with only two small curved marks or symbols visible near the top of the page. The rest of the page appears to be empty white space.

CALIFORNIA

POLITICAL REFORM AUDIT PROGRAM
745 FRANKLIN STREET, SUITE 308
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94102
TELEPHONE (415) 557-0403

June 23, 1976


Jack R. Wheatley
2240 Cowper
Palo Alto, CA 94301




The voters of California enacted the Political Reform Act in the June 1974 election
and by so doing added audits as a new dimension to the political process. A con-
firmation letter, such as this, is sent to campaign contributors, as a necessary
and routine part of an audit, to verify monetary and nonmonetary contributions
reported. This procedure is applied to all candidates, officials, and committees
that are subject to audit under the Act. No special significance should be attached
to either our sending, or your receiving, this confirmation letter.

Your name is shown as a contributor on compaign statements filed by
________________Republican Central Committee of Santa Clara County____________, for the period
______________January 7, 1975______________ through ___December 31, 1975_____________.

Your confirmation of contributions made to the above, by furnishing the information
requested below, will assist the department in the completion of its audit.

A postage-paid envelope is enclosed for your reply.


Political Reform Audit

Attach.
                                    Date          Check          Cash          Value*          Other               Description

I have not written any checks during this period to the Republican Central

Committee and do not know what contributions this committee shows in its

_______report. Wrote a $100.00 check on 9/30 (#1678) to the United Republican
Finance Committee for a fund raising dinner.
*If nonmonetary, enter estimated fair market value.
                                                            Signature:_____________________________

                                    Date:          July 5, 1976

(If more space is needed for listing, use reverse side of this form, or attach a
separate schedule.)

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with only some faint markings at the top that are not clearly legible]

embedding.qa_text: August 4, 1976 Mr. Jack R. Weatley 2240 Cowper Palo Alto, California 94301 Dear Mr. Weatley: Your letter of July 9, 1976 to Senator Arlen Gregorio has been referred to this agency for comment. The Political Reform Act of 1974, which requires the disclosure of campaign contributions and expenditures, provides that the State Franchise Tax Board shall conduct audits and field investigations in regard to campaign statements and reports. The Political Reform Act does not require the Franchise Tax Board's Political Reform Program to send "confirmation letters" to contributors such as yourself. The Franchise Tax Board, however, considers such letters to be a necessary and routine part of an audit. It should be noted that compliance with the Franchise Tax Board's Political Reform Program request for information verifying a contribution is entirely voluntary on the part of the recipient of such a "confirmation letter." We hope you were not unduly inconvenienced. Sincerely, JOHN KEPLINGER Division Chief JK:bw cc: Senator Arlen Gregorio bcc: Gina Jeanne P. This is an image of a handwritten letter, which appears to be written in English. However, the handwriting is quite faint and mostly illegible, making it difficult to transcribe accurately. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible: - "Dear Mr. Messily:" - "Your letter of July 6, 1947 to Senator Alan Gracovio" - "We have received no reply to this request for comment." - "The local fair of 1947 will be the occasion of combining competition and exhibition, pro" - "We find investigation to be going on between state and city agencies and departments." - "The Police Bureau does not require the Fair" - "Boys' however, could be very effective to be a necessary and efficient tool for community service as well." - "The Board's Policy is to remain to keep 'community Tax Fairness Tax Exhibition' free from political interference." - "It should be noted that competition with the Fair might be one basis of an angle." - "More or less conventional." - "The reception of such a confirmation letter." We hope not - "Valuing a contribution is simply voluntary on the part of Tax Board's Policy." - "Sincerely," - "Majoron Chief" - "John Kretzner" The remainder of the content cannot be reliably transcribed due to the quality and style of the handwriting. **Senator Arlen Gregorio** Tenth District San Mateo and Santa Clara Counties Committees Health and Welfare Chairman Education Elections and Reapportionment Governmental Organization July 28, 1976 Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman Fair Political Practices Commission 925 J Street Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Mr. Lowenstein: Enclosed is a letter from a constituent of Senator Gregorio relative to the necessity of his having to file the amount of his political contributions to the FPPC and what he believes to be duplication of effort. Would you be good enough to address a reply directly to him regarding this requirement. Thank you for your consideration. Sincerely, [Signature] Beth A. Labson Administrative Assistant BAL: IW The image you provided is completely white, with no visible content
